Web research on TTL OS fingerprinting standards. Multiple sources confirm: GitHub TTL-Based-OS-Detection repo states 'Cisco network devices: 255' and 'Solaris or AIX: 254'. Default TTL Values blog confirms Solaris/AIX use 254, not 255

privileged_info_summary: The task uses official Wireshark sample capture files (http.cap, dns.cap, smtp.pcap, telnet-cooked.pcap, 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng) from the Wireshark project wiki. These synthetic captures contain 268 total IP packets across 17 unique source IPs with observed TTL values of 47, 50, 55, 58, 63, 64, 128, and 249. The TTL-to-OS mapping provided (32→Windows-9x, 64→Linux/Unix, 128→Windows) is accurate per industry standards. However, the mapping of 255→Cisco/Solaris is inaccurate: the actual default TTL for Solaris/AIX is 254, not 255. Cisco devices do use 255. The task correctly implements the methodology of determining initial TTL by rounding observed TTL up to the nearest standard value (32, 64, 128, 255), then classifying OS family accordingly.
